uk: 🕃 Передсвяття Переображення Господа нашого Ісуса Христа
saints.en: Forefeast of the Transfiguration of our Lord Jesus Christ. St. Martyr Eusignius

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи, взиваю» 6 стихир: 3 передсв. і 3 мч., Сл.: мч., І нині: передсв. Прокімен дня. Стиховня передсв. По “Отче наш” тропар мч., Сл., І нині: передсв. Відпуст середній з помин. дня та мч.
Утреня: по “Бог Господь”: тропар передсв. (2), Сл.: мч., І нині: передсв. Катизми черг., сідал. передсв. Пс. 50. Канон передсв. і мч. Катавасія тільки по 3-ій, 6-ій, 8-ій та 9-ій пісні – ірмос канону мч. По 3-ій – конд.-ікос та сідал. мч., Сл., І нині: передсв. По 6-ій – конд.-ікос передсв.; по 9-ій – світил. мч., Сл., І нині: світил. передсв. Стиховня передсв., Сл.: мч., І нині: передсв. По “Отче наш”: тропар мч., Сл., І нині: передсв.
Часи: на всіх часах тропар передсв., а на 3-му та 9-му – Сл.: тр. мч. Кондаки: на 1-му та 6-му – передсв., а на 3-му та 9-му – мч.
Літургія: тропар передсв., Сл., І нині: кондак передсв. Прокімен, Ап., алилуя, Єв. та причасний дня. Ап. – 2 Кор. 173 зач.; 3, 4-11. Єв. – Мт. 96 зач.; 23, 29-39.

troparion_en.text: (Podoben: “Be quick to anticipate...“)Come, you faithful, let us meet the Transfiguration of Christ in advance,  / and celebrating the prefeast, let us cry aloud,  / “Now the day of divine gladness is at hand;  / the Master ascends Mount Tabor  / to flash forth with the beauty of His divinity.”

kontakion_en.text:
(Podoben: “Thou hast appeared today...”)
Now, beforehand, all of mortal nature divinely shines today  / with the divine Transfiguration;  / and in gladness she cries out: “Christ is
transfigured, saving all.”

matched_oca_saint: Forefeast of the Transfiguration of our Lord

oca_saints.name: Forefeast of the Transfiguration of our Lord

troparion.text: (Podoben: “Be quick to anticipate...“)Come, you faithful, let us meet the Transfiguration of Christ in advance,  / and celebrating the prefeast, let us cry aloud,  / “Now the day of divine gladness is at hand;  / the Master ascends Mount Tabor  / to flash forth with the beauty of His divinity.”

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “Thou hast appeared today...”)
Now, beforehand, all of mortal nature divinely shines today  / with the divine Transfiguration;  / and in gladness she cries out: “Christ is
transfigured, saving all.”

troparion.text: Today the Church honors a man martyred for his piety and devotion: / The sincere and Godly-minded Eusignius the Wise. / She glorifies his spiritual struggles, and cries out fervently: / O Most Merciful One, guard Your servants through his intercessions!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Eusignius;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

troparion.text: The image of God was truly preserved in you, O Father, / for you took up the Cross and followed Christ. / By so doing you taught us to disregard the flesh for it passes away / but to care instead for the soul, since it is immortal. / Therefore your spirit, venerable Job, rejoices with the angels.

By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, /  you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, / you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, / you defended the Faith, even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Antherus entreat Christ God to save our souls.

oca_saints.name: Hieromartyr Fabian, Pope of Rome, and Martyr Pontious

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Cantidius, Cantidian, and Sibelius (Sobel) of Egypt

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Theoctistus, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

Your holy martyr Pontius, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Pontius;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Oswald, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Oswald;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father John, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

Your holy martyr Christos, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Christos;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.
